meta.note: Curated starting points, not an exhaustive Code. Links resolve to Cornell LII (statute/regs) and IRS.gov (forms/pubs).

irc.title: Tax imposed
irc.desc: The section that starts it all — imposes income tax on individuals and sets the rate schedules.

irc.title: Child and dependent care credit
irc.desc: Credit for a portion of care expenses that let you (and your spouse) work.

irc.title: Child tax credit
irc.desc: $2,200 per qualifying child under 17 (OBBBA), partially refundable, phases out at higher incomes.

irc.title: Education credits (AOTC & Lifetime Learning)
irc.desc: The two education credits — AOTC for degree students (partially refundable), LLC for everything else.

irc.title: Earned income tax credit
irc.desc: Refundable credit for lower-income workers, scaled by earned income and number of qualifying children.

irc.title: Alternative minimum tax
irc.desc: Parallel tax system that recomputes income with fewer breaks; you pay the higher of regular tax or AMT.

irc.title: Gross income defined
irc.desc: "All income from whatever source derived" — the broadest sentence in the Code and the default answer to "is this taxable?"

irc.title: Adjusted gross income defined
irc.desc: Lists the above-the-line deductions that get you from gross income to AGI.

irc.title: Taxable income defined
irc.desc: AGI minus the standard deduction or itemized deductions; where the standard deduction lives.

irc.title: Annuities & early distributions
irc.desc: Taxation of annuity payments and the 10% additional tax on early retirement distributions (§72(t)).

irc.title: Group-term life insurance
irc.desc: Why employer-paid life insurance over $50,000 of coverage shows up as income on your W-2.

irc.title: Property transferred for services
irc.desc: Governs restricted stock and the famous §83(b) election to be taxed at grant instead of vesting.

irc.title: Social Security benefits
irc.desc: The formula that makes up to 85% of Social Security benefits taxable depending on provisional income.

irc.title: Life insurance proceeds
irc.desc: Death benefits are generally excluded from income — with transfer-for-value exceptions.

irc.title: Damages for injury or sickness
irc.desc: Physical-injury damages are excludable; punitive damages and emotional-distress-only awards usually are not.

irc.title: Cancellation of debt income
irc.desc: Forgiven debt is income — unless an exclusion (bankruptcy, insolvency, qualified real property) applies.

irc.title: Home sale exclusion
irc.desc: Exclude up to $250K ($500K MFJ) of gain on your principal residence with 2-of-5-year ownership and use.

irc.title: Dependent defined
irc.desc: The qualifying-child and qualifying-relative tests that determine who counts as a dependent.

irc.title: Trade or business expenses
irc.desc: The "ordinary and necessary" standard behind nearly every business deduction.

irc.title: Interest deduction
irc.desc: Mortgage interest rules, the §163(j) business interest limitation, and investment interest limits.

irc.title: State and local taxes (SALT)
irc.desc: The SALT deduction — capped at $40,000 for 2025 (escalating, with income phasedown) under OBBBA.

irc.title: Losses
irc.desc: Deductible losses including casualty/theft rules and the worthless-securities provision.

irc.title: Charitable contributions
irc.desc: Deduction rules, AGI percentage limits, and substantiation requirements for gifts to charity.

irc.title: Expensing election
irc.desc: Immediate expensing of qualifying property — $2.5M limit with $4M phase-out threshold under OBBBA.

irc.title: Hobby loss rules
irc.desc: Activities not engaged in for profit — the 3-of-5-year presumption and the factors test.

irc.title: Start-up expenditures
irc.desc: $5,000 first-year deduction for start-up costs, remainder amortized over 180 months.

irc.title: Qualified business income deduction
irc.desc: The 20% pass-through deduction, made permanent by OBBBA — with W-2/UBIA limits and SSTB phase-outs above thresholds.

irc.title: Medical expenses
irc.desc: Itemized deduction for unreimbursed medical costs above 7.5% of AGI.

irc.title: Alimony (pre-2019 divorces)
irc.desc: Alimony is deductible/includible only for agreements executed before 2019 — TCJA flipped the rule after.

irc.title: IRA deduction
irc.desc: Traditional IRA deduction rules and the active-participant income phase-outs.

irc.title: Student loan interest
irc.desc: Above-the-line deduction up to $2,500, phased out at moderate incomes.

irc.title: Health savings accounts
irc.desc: The triple-tax-advantaged account: deductible in, tax-free growth, tax-free out for medical.

irc.title: Personal expenses
irc.desc: The blunt rule: personal, living, and family expenses are not deductible unless a section says otherwise.

irc.title: Related-party transactions
irc.desc: Loss disallowance and timing rules for transactions between related taxpayers.

irc.title: Meals, entertainment & substantiation
irc.desc: Entertainment is out, business meals are 50%, and the strict substantiation rules live here.

irc.title: Home office & vacation rentals
irc.desc: Home office requirements, the exclusive-use test, and the 14-day "Augusta rule" rental exclusion.

irc.title: Corporate distributions
irc.desc: Distribution ordering: dividend to E&P, then basis recovery, then capital gain.

irc.title: Stock redemptions
irc.desc: When a redemption is treated as a sale (capital gain) versus a dividend.

irc.title: Distributions of property
irc.desc: Corporations recognize gain (but not loss) when distributing appreciated property.

irc.title: Dividend defined
irc.desc: Defines dividends by reference to current and accumulated earnings and profits.

irc.title: Complete liquidations
irc.desc: Shareholder-level treatment when a corporation fully liquidates.

irc.title: Incorporation transfers
irc.desc: Tax-free transfer of property to a corporation for stock when transferors control 80%+.

irc.title: Spin-offs
irc.desc: Tax-free corporate separations — spin-offs, split-offs, split-ups — with strict requirements.

irc.title: Reorganizations
irc.desc: The alphabet of tax-free reorganizations: A (mergers) through G (bankruptcy).

irc.title: NOL limits after ownership change
irc.desc: Limits use of loss carryforwards after a 50%+ ownership shift — central to M&A modeling.

irc.title: Qualified retirement plans
irc.desc: The rules that make 401(k) plans "qualified" — including elective deferral mechanics.

irc.title: Individual retirement accounts
irc.desc: IRA mechanics; §408A covers Roth IRAs.

irc.title: Nonqualified deferred compensation
irc.desc: Strict timing rules for deferred comp — violations trigger immediate tax plus 20% penalty.

irc.title: Taxable year
irc.desc: Rules for adopting and defining a taxable year.

irc.title: Accounting methods
irc.desc: General rule for methods of accounting and the clear-reflection-of-income standard.

irc.title: Timing of income
irc.desc: When income is recognized — including the book-conformity rule for accrual taxpayers.

irc.title: Timing of deductions & excess business losses
irc.desc: All-events test, economic performance, and the §461(l) excess business loss limitation.

irc.title: At-risk rules
irc.desc: Second hurdle in the loss limitation waterfall — losses limited to amounts economically at risk.

irc.title: Passive activity losses
irc.desc: Third hurdle — passive losses only offset passive income; real estate professional and material participation tests live here.

irc.title: Accounting method changes
irc.desc: The catch-up adjustment (§481(a)) when a taxpayer changes accounting methods.

irc.title: Exempt organizations
irc.desc: Tax exemption for charities and other organizations — §501(c)(3) and its siblings.

irc.title: Education savings plans
irc.desc: Tax-free growth for education; OBBBA expanded qualifying K-12 and credentialing uses.

irc.title: Personal holding company tax
irc.desc: Penalty tax on closely held corporations with passive income — a trap for incorporated portfolios.

irc.title: Timber, coal & iron ore
irc.desc: Capital gain treatment elections for timber and mineral dispositions.

irc.title: Trusts & estates — imposition of tax
irc.desc: Fiduciary income taxation begins here; trusts hit the top bracket fast.

irc.title: Distribution deduction (complex trusts)
irc.desc: How distributable net income (DNI) carries income out to beneficiaries.

irc.title: Income in respect of a decedent
irc.desc: IRD — income earned before death but taxed to whoever receives it, with no basis step-up.

irc.title: Partners, not partnership, taxed
irc.desc: The foundational rule: partnerships are pass-throughs; partners pay the tax.

irc.title: Partner's distributive share
irc.desc: Items that pass through separately to partners and keep their character.

irc.title: Allocations & substantial economic effect
irc.desc: §704(b) allocation rules and §704(c) built-in gain allocations — the heart of partnership tax.

irc.title: Partner's outside basis
irc.desc: The basis rollforward: contributions and income up; distributions and losses down.

irc.title: Partnership taxable years
irc.desc: Required tax years and closing-of-the-year rules when interests change.

irc.title: Partner-partnership transactions
irc.desc: Guaranteed payments, disguised sales, and transactions with partners acting as non-partners.

irc.title: Contributions to partnership
irc.desc: Generally tax-free contribution of property for a partnership interest — with investment-company exceptions.

irc.title: Basis of contributing partner
irc.desc: Contributing partner takes an outside basis equal to money plus adjusted basis of property contributed.

irc.title: Partnership distributions
irc.desc: Distributions are generally tax-free until cash exceeds outside basis.

irc.title: Basis of distributed property
irc.desc: Carryover and substituted basis rules for property received in distributions.

irc.title: Basis adjustment — distributions
irc.desc: Inside-basis adjustment on distributions when a §754 election is in place.

irc.title: Retiring partner payments
irc.desc: Classifies liquidating payments to retiring partners as §736(a) income or §736(b) property payments.

irc.title: Sale of partnership interest
irc.desc: Capital gain on sale of an interest — except for §751 hot assets.

irc.title: Basis adjustment — transfers
irc.desc: §743(b) inside-basis step-up for the buyer of a partnership interest when §754 is elected.

irc.title: Hot assets
irc.desc: Unrealized receivables and inventory that convert capital gain to ordinary income on sales and distributions.

irc.title: Partnership liabilities
irc.desc: Liability allocations treated as deemed contributions/distributions — recourse vs. nonrecourse rules.

irc.title: Basis adjustment election
irc.desc: The election that switches on §743(b) and §734(b) inside-basis adjustments.

irc.title: Partnership definitions
irc.desc: What counts as a partnership, and the election out of subchapter K.

irc.title: U.S.-source income rules
irc.desc: Sourcing rules that drive foreign tax credit limits and nonresident taxation.

irc.title: Nonresident alien taxation
irc.desc: 30% withholding on FDAP income and graduated rates on effectively connected income.

irc.title: Expatriation exit tax
irc.desc: Mark-to-market exit tax for covered expatriates giving up citizenship or long-term residency.

irc.title: Foreign tax credit
irc.desc: Credit for foreign income taxes paid — subject to the §904 limitation.

irc.title: Foreign earned income exclusion
irc.desc: Exclude foreign wages ($132,900 for 2026) under the bona fide residence or physical presence tests.

irc.title: Subpart F income
irc.desc: Immediate U.S. taxation of certain CFC income regardless of distribution.

irc.title: GILTI (now NCTI)
irc.desc: Global minimum tax on CFC earnings — renamed Net CFC Tested Income with revised rates under OBBBA.

irc.title: Controlled foreign corporation defined
irc.desc: The >50% U.S.-shareholder ownership test that triggers the CFC regime.

irc.title: Foreign currency transactions
irc.desc: Ordinary treatment of exchange gain/loss on §988 transactions.

irc.title: Gain or loss on dispositions
irc.desc: Amount realized minus adjusted basis — the fundamental gain equation.

irc.title: Cost basis
irc.desc: Basis of property is its cost — the starting point for everything.

irc.title: Basis step-up at death
irc.desc: Inherited property takes fair-market-value basis — the most powerful basis rule in the Code.

irc.title: Basis of gifts
irc.desc: Carryover basis for gifts, with the dual-basis rule for loss property.

irc.title: Like-kind exchanges
irc.desc: Defer gain on exchanges of real property held for business or investment.

irc.title: Wash sales
irc.desc: Loss disallowed if you rebuy substantially identical securities within 30 days.

irc.title: Qualified small business stock
irc.desc: Exclude up to $15M (OBBBA) of gain on QSBS — with new tiered holding periods from 3 years.

irc.title: Capital loss limitation
irc.desc: Individuals deduct net capital losses against only $3,000 of ordinary income per year.

irc.title: Capital asset defined
irc.desc: Everything is a capital asset except the listed exclusions (inventory, receivables, etc.).

irc.title: Business property gains & losses
irc.desc: Best of both worlds: net gains are capital, net losses are ordinary.

irc.title: Depreciation recapture — personalty
irc.desc: Gain on depreciated equipment is ordinary to the extent of depreciation taken.

irc.title: Depreciation recapture — realty
irc.desc: Unrecaptured §1250 gain on real estate taxed at up to 25%.

irc.title: S corporation defined
irc.desc: Eligibility: ≤100 shareholders, one class of stock, permitted shareholders only.

irc.title: S election & termination
irc.desc: Making, revoking, and inadvertently blowing the S election.

irc.title: Pass-through to shareholders
irc.desc: Items flow through pro-rata; losses limited to stock plus debt basis.

irc.title: Shareholder basis adjustments
irc.desc: The S-corp basis rollforward tracked on Form 7203.

irc.title: S corporation distributions
irc.desc: Distribution ordering through AAA, E&P, and basis — the source of most S-corp surprises.

irc.title: Built-in gains tax
irc.desc: Corporate-level tax on appreciation from C-corp years, recognized within 5 years of election.

irc.title: Self-employment tax
irc.desc: The 15.3% SECA tax on net self-employment earnings.

irc.title: Net earnings from self-employment
irc.desc: Defines what's subject to SE tax — including the limited partner exception debates.

irc.title: Net investment income tax
irc.desc: The 3.8% NIIT on investment income above $200K/$250K MAGI thresholds.

irc.title: Estate tax unified credit
irc.desc: The basic exclusion amount — $15M per person for 2026 under OBBBA.

irc.title: Annual gift exclusion
irc.desc: Gifts up to $19,000 per donee (2026) require no return and use no exemption.

irc.title: Requirement to file
irc.desc: The general duty to file returns and the digital-asset reporting hooks.

irc.title: Partnership returns
irc.desc: Partnership information return (Form 1065) filing requirement.

irc.title: Foreign corporation reporting
irc.desc: The statute behind Form 5471 — and its $10,000-per-form penalties.

irc.title: Filing deadlines
irc.desc: Statutory due dates: April 15 for individuals, March 15 for partnerships and S-corps.

irc.title: Extensions of time to file
irc.desc: Authority for the automatic 6-month extension — to file, not to pay.

irc.title: Refunds and offsets
irc.desc: Authority for refunds and the government's right to offset them against debts.

irc.title: Assessment statute of limitations
irc.desc: 3 years generally, 6 for substantial omissions, unlimited for fraud or no return.

irc.title: Refund claim deadlines
irc.desc: Claim within 3 years of filing or 2 years of payment, whichever is later.

irc.title: Interest on underpayments
irc.desc: Interest runs from the original due date regardless of extensions.

irc.title: Failure to file / pay penalties
irc.desc: 5%/month for late filing, 0.5%/month for late payment — and the reasonable-cause defense.

irc.title: Estimated tax penalty
irc.desc: Underpayment penalty and the 90%/100%/110% safe harbors.

irc.title: Accuracy-related penalty
irc.desc: 20% penalty for negligence or substantial understatement — with substantial-authority defenses.

irc.title: Trust fund recovery penalty
irc.desc: Personal liability for responsible persons who fail to remit withheld payroll taxes.

irc.title: Preparer penalties
irc.desc: Penalties on paid preparers for unreasonable positions — why preparers ask hard questions.

irc.title: Closing agreements
irc.desc: Final and binding agreements with the IRS.

irc.title: Offers in compromise
irc.desc: Statutory authority to settle tax debts for less than the full amount.

irc.title: Timely mailed, timely filed
irc.desc: The postmark rule that saves last-minute filers.

irc.title: Definitions & check-the-box
irc.desc: Master definitions, entity classification, and the substance-over-form backstops.

irc.title: IRS Commissioner & Taxpayer Advocate
irc.desc: IRS governance and the Taxpayer Bill of Rights codification.

regs.title: Gross income — general
regs.desc: Income realized in any form: money, property, or services.

regs.title: §83(b) election mechanics
regs.desc: The 30-day election filing requirements — miss it and there's no fix.

regs.title: Business expenses
regs.desc: What "ordinary and necessary" means in practice.

regs.title: Hobby loss factors
regs.desc: The nine-factor profit-motive test courts actually apply.

regs.title: QBI deduction operational rules
regs.desc: How to compute the §199A deduction, aggregate businesses, and apply the limits.

regs.title: SSTB definitions
regs.desc: Which service businesses lose the QBI deduction above the thresholds.

regs.title: Material participation tests
regs.desc: The seven tests (500 hours, etc.) that make an activity non-passive.

regs.title: Substantial economic effect
regs.desc: The capital account, liquidation, and deficit-restoration rules that validate special allocations.

regs.title: §704(c) methods
regs.desc: Traditional, curative, and remedial methods for built-in gain allocations.

regs.title: §743(b) adjustment mechanics
regs.desc: Computing and allocating the inside-basis step-up on interest transfers.

regs.title: Liability allocation framework
regs.desc: Recourse vs. nonrecourse and the economic-risk-of-loss analysis.

regs.title: S corporation eligibility
regs.desc: One-class-of-stock rules and shareholder eligibility detail.

regs.title: Net investment income definition
regs.desc: What counts as NII, including the trade-or-business exceptions.

regs.title: Check-the-box elections
regs.desc: Entity classification elections — the regulation behind Form 8832.

regs.title: Reportable transactions
regs.desc: Disclosure rules for listed and reportable transactions (Form 8886).

forms.title: U.S. Individual Income Tax Return
forms.desc: The individual return everything else attaches to.

forms.title: Nonresident Alien Return
forms.desc: For nonresidents — including most international students in their first 5 years.

forms.title: Estimated Tax for Individuals
forms.desc: Quarterly estimated payment vouchers and worksheet.

forms.title: Amended Return
forms.desc: Fix a filed return — generally within 3 years.

forms.title: Wage and Tax Statement
forms.desc: Employer-reported wages and withholding.

forms.title: Withholding Certificate
forms.desc: Controls paycheck withholding — the fix for big refunds or surprise bills.

forms.title: Request for TIN
forms.desc: What clients send before issuing you a 1099.

forms.title: Nonemployee Compensation
forms.desc: Freelance and contractor income reporting.

forms.title: Payment Card / Third-Party Network
forms.desc: Payment-app and marketplace reporting.

forms.title: Profit or Loss from Business
forms.desc: Sole proprietor and single-member LLC income and expenses.

forms.title: Capital Gains and Losses
forms.desc: Summarizes capital transactions from Form 8949.

forms.title: Rental, Royalty & K-1 Income
forms.desc: Rentals and pass-through income land here.

forms.title: Sales of Capital Assets
forms.desc: Transaction-level detail behind Schedule D.

forms.title: Depreciation and Amortization
forms.desc: §179, bonus, and MACRS depreciation reporting.

forms.title: Home Office Expenses
forms.desc: The actual-expense home office computation.

forms.title: Underpayment of Estimated Tax
forms.desc: Computes the §6654 penalty and safe-harbor tests.

forms.title: Extension to File
forms.desc: Automatic 6-month filing extension — payment still due April 15.

forms.title: Installment Agreement Request
forms.desc: Set up an IRS payment plan.

forms.title: Partnership Return
forms.desc: Partnership information return, due March 15.

forms.title: S Corporation Return
forms.desc: S-corp return with shareholder K-1s.

forms.title: C Corporation Return
forms.desc: Corporate income tax return — flat 21% rate.

forms.title: S Corp Shareholder Basis
forms.desc: Required stock and debt basis tracking for S-corp shareholders.

forms.title: Entity Classification Election
forms.desc: The check-the-box election form.

forms.title: S Election
forms.desc: Elect S status — due 2.5 months into the year (late relief exists).

forms.title: Foreign Corporation Information Return
forms.desc: U.S. persons with interests in foreign corporations; $10K+ penalties for misses.

forms.title: Foreign-Owned U.S. Corporation
forms.desc: Reporting for 25% foreign-owned U.S. entities, including foreign-owned SMLLCs.

forms.title: FATCA Foreign Assets
forms.desc: Specified foreign financial assets above thresholds — filed with the 1040.

forms.title: PFIC Reporting
forms.desc: Passive foreign investment companies — foreign mutual funds' painful cousin.

forms.title: FBAR
forms.desc: Foreign accounts over $10K aggregate — filed with FinCEN, not the IRS.

forms.title: Power of Attorney
forms.desc: Authorize a representative before the IRS.

forms.title: Gift Tax Return
forms.desc: Report gifts above the annual exclusion; usually no tax due.

forms.title: Estate Tax Return
forms.desc: Estate tax and the portability election for the deceased spouse's exclusion.

forms.title: Quarterly Payroll Return
forms.desc: Employer withholding and FICA reporting.

forms.title: Premium Tax Credit
forms.desc: Reconciles ACA marketplace subsidies.

forms.title: HSA Reporting
forms.desc: HSA contributions and distributions.

pubs.title: Your Federal Income Tax
pubs.desc: The IRS's own general guide to individual returns — the closest thing to an official manual.

pubs.title: Travel, Gift & Car Expenses
pubs.desc: Substantiation and deduction rules for business travel and vehicles.

pubs.title: Dependents & Filing Status
pubs.desc: Who must file, who counts as a dependent, which status applies.

pubs.title: Withholding & Estimated Tax
pubs.desc: The safe-harbor rules and estimated payment mechanics.

pubs.title: U.S. Tax Guide for Aliens
pubs.desc: Residency tests, treaties, and dual-status years — essential for international students.

pubs.title: Taxable & Nontaxable Income
pubs.desc: The long list of what is and isn't income.

pubs.title: Charitable Contributions
pubs.desc: Deduction limits and substantiation for donations.

pubs.title: Investment Income & Expenses
pubs.desc: Interest, dividends, wash sales, and basis rules for investors.

pubs.title: Basis of Assets
pubs.desc: Cost, gift, and inherited basis rules in one place.

pubs.title: IRA Contributions
pubs.desc: Contribution limits, deductibility phase-outs, and Roth eligibility.

pubs.title: IRA Distributions
pubs.desc: RMD rules, early-withdrawal exceptions, and inherited IRA regimes.

pubs.title: How to Depreciate Property
pubs.desc: MACRS tables, conventions, §179 and bonus depreciation.

pubs.title: Tax Benefits for Education
pubs.desc: AOTC, LLC, 529 plans, and student loan interest in one guide.
